title: Linux Encrypted Channel C2 Communication
description: Detects establishment of encrypted communication channels that may indicate C2 activity. Attackers use OpenSSL, stunnel, and encrypted reverse shells to establish covert command and control over TLS/SSL.
query: ('Log Source' = 'SOC Linux Syslog Logs' or 'Log Source' = 'Linux Secure Logs' or 'Log Source' = 'Linux Syslog Logs' or 'Log Source' = 'Linux Audit Logs') and (((msg like '*openssl s_client -connect*' or msg like '*openssl s_server*')) or ((msg like '*stunnel*' or msg like '*stunnel.conf*')) or ((msg like '*ncat --ssl*' or msg like '*socat openssl-connect*' or msg like '*socat OPENSSL*')) or ((msg like '*ssh -R*' or msg like '*ssh -fNR*')))
